cpu_summary: 2020 Zen 3 16-core enthusiast desktop; AMD's flagship consumer CPU at launch, still strong in 2026.
quant_kernel_note: Zen 3 (2020): no AVX-VNNI (added in Zen 4, 2022). ggml int8 path uses the AVX2 fallback. Quant speedup vs fp16 is bandwidth-only here; on 16 fast cores + dual-channel DDR4 the headroom is large enough that quant still wins, but not by the VNNI multiplier.
gpu: 0a:00.0 VGA compatible controller [0300]: NVIDIA Corporation AD102 [GeForce RTX 4090] [10de:2684] (rev a1)

gpu_build.reason: Vulkan sweep run from the Proxmox host (proxmox4, 192.168.0.74, PVE kernel 6.17.13-9-pve) where the RTX 4090 is visible via NVIDIA driver 595.71.05 and exposed to Vulkan as PHYSICAL_DEVICE_TYPE_DISCRETE_GPU. Prior 'skipped' state was inside the LXC container `ai` (192.168.0.79) which never received GPU passthrough; same silicon, different OS namespace. CPU-only quant sweep was re-run on the same host with a separately-built fono-bench compiled without the accel-vulkan feature so the kernels actually execute on CPU.

whisper_thread_cap_reason: 32 logical threads oversubscribe whisper.cpp matmul kernels by ~20x on short clips; capped to physical core count
